name: Val-de-Marne (France)
description: A gold field bearing a large blue inverted triangle issuing from the chief, strewn with gold fleurs-de-lis and crossed by two wavy white bands meeting in a point at the base.
history: The fleurs-de-lis recall the royal arms of France, with the wavy bands evoking the confluence of the Marne and the Seine.

features.role: two wavy bands meeting at base
